MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-26-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SARAH BEARSE'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant's motion to strike portions of plaintiff's first amended complaint is denied. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts showing malice to support punitive damages. "[M]alice does not require actual intent to harm. Conscious disregard for the safety of another may be sufficient where the defendant is aware of the probable dangerous consequences of his or her conduct and he or she willfully fails to avoid such consequences." (Pfeifer v. John Crane, Inc. (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 1270, 1299 (citation omitted); see also College Hospital, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 8 Cal.4th 704, 713). In paragraph 23 of the first Amended complaint plaintiff alleges that Rico had bitten two other individuals before biting plaintiff. Paragraphs 24, 27 and 57 allege that defendant had knowledge of these prior attacks which posed a threat to the public, yet defendant continued to let Rico roam off leash. These allegations are sufficient to defeat a pleading motion.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/HEK)
